scope_boundary: Public-safe Project Theseus work-board metadata import only. The sanitized fixture records a pinned dirty-checkout work-board executor snapshot with 130 durable task rows, 412 event rows, 133 evidence rows, 5 SQLite tables, 1 execution-ledger row, 4 unattended-improvement rows, 72 feedback rows, zero public training rows, zero external inference calls, ten expected-invalid controls, and a stale-snapshot boundary. It does not copy raw reports, SQLite rows, task payloads, private paths, prompts, tests, solutions, score labels, candidate code, checkpoints, dogfood traces, or training rows into the public book. It does not rerun Project Theseus, execute a board step, prove clean live Project Theseus replay, or create an upward support-state transition.
transition_reason: The work-board import is useful because it makes the durable task/state substrate reviewable by digest, aggregate counts, SQLite table counts, ledger counts, public-data rules, currentness limits, and negative controls. It remains a stale metadata-only snapshot from a dirty checkout, so it blocks promotion for clean live Theseus replay, current dashboard state, current board state, deployed behavior, model quality, benchmark superiority, generation speed, useful-solution-per-second improvement, unattended safety, self-evolution safety, support-state movement, ASI, or chapter-core claims.
required_artifacts: clean Project Theseus replay from a public-safe checkout or archived public release fixture
fresh work-board status refresh with environment notes and exact command
publication permission or redaction record for any broader work-board artifact
artifact-truth review for referenced board rows and ledgers
missing-artifact closure or durable gap ledger with reviewer acceptance
negative_results: clean live Project Theseus replay remains unclaimed
fresh currentness remains unclaimed because the imported status is a stale snapshot
source checkout was dirty at import time
raw reports, SQLite rows, task payloads, private paths, prompts, tests, solutions, score labels, candidate code, checkpoints, dogfood traces, and training rows are not copied into this repository
no chapter core claim is promoted
no upward support-state transition is created
downgrade_triggers: raw report, SQLite row, task payload, private path, prompt, test, solution, score label, candidate-code, checkpoint, dogfood trace, or training-row leakage
fresh-currentness overclaim accepted from stale snapshot
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed Project Theseus work-board claim after a clean public-safe replay or archived public fixture, fresh status refresh, environment notes, publication-permission closure, artifact-truth review for referenced rows and ledgers, missing-artifact closure, independent review, and a separate accepted upward transition. Metadata-only work-board evidence by itself blocks promotion.
limitations: dirty local source checkout at import time
raw reports and SQLite/task payloads not copied into the public repository
no clean live Project Theseus replay
no current dashboard or current board-state proof
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent Project Theseus, benchmark, model-quality, verifier-quality, source-interpretation, artifact-truth, dashboard-state, work-board-state, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: dirty source checkout at import time
no raw-report or SQLite-row publication permission
non_claims: does not promote Project Theseus above argument

does not prove clean live Project Theseus replay, current dashboard state, current board state, deployed Theseus behavior, model quality, benchmark superiority, generation speed, useful-solution-per-second improvement, support-state movement, unattended safety, self-evolution safety, deployment readiness, ASI, or chapter-core promotion
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote the Project Theseus chapter core claim
does not promote any chapter core claim
